column1: # price-table.tsv — consumer config for bin/stage-economics.sh (drift-kit/SPEC.md §The stage-economics meter).
# Columns are USD per single token (a $/MTok price / 1,000,000), tab-separated.
# One row per model id as it appears in a transcript's message.model.
# priced-as-of: 2026-07-20 — transcribed from the published Anthropic model-pricing
# table. Prices are a dated literal with no machine-readable feed to derive them from,
# so this file goes stale silently; re-verify against the published table when the
# staleness advisory raises it.
# cache_creation encodes the 1-HOUR cache-write price (2x base input). Sessions here
# run a 1h prompt-cache TTL; the 5-minute write (1.25x base input) applies only when
# a session drops to the short TTL, so a mixed-TTL corpus prices cache_creation high.
# Cache reads dominate the totals by one to two orders of magnitude, so the convention
# moves the result far less than the cache_read column does.
# KNOWN CLIFF — 2026-09-01: the Sonnet 5 row is INTRODUCTORY pricing, in effect through
# input 0.000003, output 0.000015, cache_read 0.0000003, cache_creation 0.000006.
# That moves the Opus:Sonnet ratio from 2.5x to ~1.67x on every column — any tier
# comparison run after that date against these numbers overstates the saving.
# model
claude-fable-5
claude-opus-4-8
claude-sonnet-5
claude-haiku-4-5
